content.full_text:
January 10, 1989

Mr. John Rothman Esquire  
Legal Division  
State of California  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J. Street Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Rothman:

This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conversation at 3:02 p.m. this date, concerning questions regarding my Client who is an unannounced Candidate for Sheriff of a major Southern California Agency.

My question concerned if under those Statutes that you are responsible for, is there any prohibition for him not to wear, or display himself in a U.S. Marine Corp Uniform with an Actor that is currently portraying the role of Marine Corp Officer on Television.

The purpose of the wearing of a Uniform is intended to appeal to the large Marine Constituency that exist there.

The Client is a Marine Corp Reservist, and has permission to wear it from his Reserve Command.

My Concerns were over the 1979 Election for State Attorney General wherein Sheriff John Duffy of San Diego County made a television endorsement wearing his Uniform that was contested by the opposition Candidate Ms. Burke.

As I recall you stated that it was not a violation of FPPC Regulations, or State Law, is this correct?

Sincerely,

ROBERT A. DANFORD
